description: Hive discusses leadership and S.H.I.E.L.D.'s role in global conflict with Daisy before overseeing Dr. Radcliffe's attempt to transform Hydra loyalists into Inhumans. Daisy asks about the process, and Radcliffe explains the procedure using Terrigen Crystals. The experiment goes awry, proving fatal to the subjects and intensifying Hive’s frustration with Radcliffe’s scientific limitations.

transcript.line: This town represents the worst of humanity, allowing the greed of a few to destroy the needs of many. Their water was poisoned, which drove them out. The great failing was their unwillingness to band together.
Sometimes it's hard to join forces. You need a good leader.
Like Coulson?
He was the reason why I joined S.H.I.E.L.D.
S.H.I.E.L.D. is no better than those Kree Reapers, both making soldiers for their never-ending wars.
My friends at S.H.I.E.L.D. wanted to stop wars, not start them.
By building giant battleships floating in the sky? The only reason S.H.I.E.L.D. exists is to fight wars.
But isn't that why we exist? I mean, that's what the Kree Reapers were hoping to make, right? Soldiers.
Which is not what I became.
Sometimes, the world's greatest miracles happen by accident.
The world I was born into was primitive. It has evolved at a remarkable pace while I was away.
So, what happened back then, to get you kicked off of Earth?
People saw the connection I had with other Inhumans, and that made them afraid. That fear bred paranoia, which led to civil war.
Not much has changed, really.
Only now we have the ability to re-create the Kree experiment. Are the patients ready, Dr. Radcliffe?
Yes, not that you did us any favors with your alien-abduction story. Took a good hour to assure them there'd be no probes of any sort.
Well, your method is much more elegant.
Not to mention more efficient, now that I've added the Terrigen Crystals to the prokaryotic compound. W...
You can change their DNA and turn them Inhuman, all at the same time?
Ah, the old one-two punch. That particular bit of ingenuity was my idea. So... to the moment at hand.

The world fears our kind of power because not everyone has it. Only billionaires can build iron suits. Only the military can make super soldiers, which can only lead to a war of its own, whereas I plan to apply a more, uh, collectivist philosophy.
Does, uh, does that mean we can get started?
Yes.
Right. Great.
Oh, God, please, make it stop!
